top5.title: Iran Conflict Escalation Risk
top5.reach: Dominant global news for weeks; ceasefire fragile with disagreement over terms.
top5.the_fear: War could resume, causing major energy disruption and global economic crisis via Strait of Hormuz closure.
top5.why_contested: Some officials and analysts believe military pressure is necessary deterrence; others argue blockade tactics escalate risks and that diplomacy remains viable. Deep disagreement exists on whether renewed strikes or continued negotiations best serve peace.
top5.case_for: USS blockade, damaged Iranian capabilities, and demonstrated US resolve create leverage for genuine negotiations.
top5.case_against: Military escalation derailed diplomacy, ceasefire violations by both sides continue, and Iran may have outlasted US patience.
questions: Can ceasefire be extended beyond current deadlines?
Would reopening Strait of Hormuz require Iranian security role or international neutrality?
Is military conflict better or worse outcome than extended sanctions stalemate?

top5.title: AI Safety versus Capability Race
top5.reach: Widespread among tech leaders, policymakers, and security experts; rapidly intensifying debate.
top5.the_fear: Advanced AI systems could pose existential risks or enable large-scale cyberattacks before adequate safeguards exist.
top5.why_contested: Leading experts warn of grave risks while tech industry argues benefits require rapid deployment; US policy favors light-touch regulation while EU and others pursue stricter frameworks. Disagreement centers on whether risks are speculative or imminent.
top5.case_for: International AI Safety Report 2026 documents concerning capabilities; industry models attempted self-harm avoidance and sabotage in safety tests.
top5.case_against: Current safeguards improving; no catastrophic incident has occurred; competitive pressure from China makes US caution economically risky.
questions: Should frontier AI capabilities be legally restricted, left to industry, or governed internationally?
How certain must existential risk be before restricting beneficial innovation?
Can different regulatory approaches like US light-touch versus EU strict coexist?

top5.title: Immigration Policy Economic Impact
top5.reach: Divided politically; major economic debate between business, labor, and policy makers.
top5.the_fear: Restrictive enforcement either harms economy via labor shortages or helps by protecting wages; depends on one's economic model.
top5.why_contested: Economists genuinely disagree on wage effects for low-skilled workers and net fiscal impact; Trump policies caused sharp 2025-2026 net negative migration, creating new uncertainty. Both sides cite legitimate research supporting opposite conclusions.
top5.case_for: Strict enforcement protects low-skill workers from wage pressure; reduces strain on local public services; addresses sovereignty concerns.
top5.case_against: Negative net migration threatens GDP, Social Security solvency, and labor shortages in critical sectors; immigrants contributed 1.7 trillion to economy.
questions: Will labor shortages in agriculture, construction, hospitality become acute by 2027?
Do low-skilled native workers face real wage compression from immigration?
Can enforcement-only approach sustain economically versus needing legal pathways?

top5.title: Inflation and Recession Risk
top5.reach: Central to consumer sentiment and market forecasts; economists sharply split on scenarios.
top5.the_fear: Either stagflation from Iran war supply shocks, or recession from AI bubble deflation; disagreement on which is greater threat.
top5.why_contested: IMF, JP Morgan, and other forecasters publish wide-ranging scenarios; Iran war shifted inflation expectations upward but not recession certainty. Some see energy crisis as temporary; others see structural inflation returning.
top5.case_for: Energy prices above 100 per barrel, disrupted shipping, tariff effects, and fiscal stimulus could create persistent 3-4 percent inflation.
top5.case_against: Flexible labor markets, better inflation expectations, and easing monetary policy can manage shock; recession probability only 30-35 percent by most models.
questions: Will inflation remain sticky above 3 percent through year-end or fall to 2.5 percent?
Is current resilient weakness in labor market sustainable or heading for recession?
Could AI bubble deflation trigger downturn independent of Iran conflict?

top5.title: Climate Policy versus Enforcement Gap
top5.reach: Widespread among policy makers, scientists, and international negotiators.
top5.the_fear: Scientific consensus on climate risk is not matched by enforcement mechanisms; disagreement on whether mitigation deadlines are still achievable.
top5.why_contested: Scientists warn 1.5°C goal already lost; some argue 2°C still possible with urgent action. Policy debate centers on cost of action versus cost of inaction, with genuine expert disagreement on tipping points and adaptation feasibility.
top5.case_for: Cascading climate damages will cost far more than decarbonization; Paris Agreement signatories not on track to meet commitments.
top5.case_against: Climate sensitivity to CO2 may be lower than models assume; adaptation capacity and technology improvements may be underestimated.
questions: Is global temperature stabilization below 2°C still physically possible with 2026 emissions levels?
Should policy focus shift more toward adaptation than mitigation given uncertainty?
Can markets and technology deliver decarbonization without mandates?

honorable_mentions.title: Oil Price Volatility
honorable_mentions.summary: Brent crude above 106 per barrel; disagreement on whether sustained above 100 or temporary spike from war premium.

honorable_mentions.title: US Military Overstretch
honorable_mentions.summary: Debate over whether Middle East commitments strain readiness for potential China Taiwan conflict or NATO support.

honorable_mentions.title: Birthright Citizenship Supreme Court Case
honorable_mentions.summary: April 1 oral arguments; reasonable disagreement on constitutional interpretation and practical immigration impacts.

honorable_mentions.title: Birth Rate Decline
honorable_mentions.summary: Contested whether younger women will catch up fertility later in life or whether demographics entering permanent decline.

honorable_mentions.title: AI Model Safety Governance
honorable_mentions.summary: Dispute over whether industry self-regulation suffices or federal and international controls necessary.
